business.headline: Air India to cut international flights till July amid fuel surge, airspace curbsAir India will cut international flights from June to July as rising ATF prices, airspace restrictions, and longer routes make several operations unprofitable, CEO Campbell Wilson said.

How best to tackle oil shock? Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy respondsIn an interview with India Today, Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy, Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the global energy shock and its impact on the Indian economy following the recent hikes in commercial LPG and anticipated domestic fuel price increases.

Not complying with trade deal: Trump hikes tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%The move comes months after Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to a new trade deal in July 2025, which set a 15 per cent tariff on most goods, including automobiles and auto parts.

Petrol, diesel prices may be hiked by Rs 4-5 per litre, LPG by Rs 40-50: SourcesPetrol and diesel prices may increase by Rs four to five per litre, while domestic LPG cylinder prices could rise by Rs 40-50 per cylinder, according to government sources.

Fuel, LPG prices may go up, decision likely within 5-7 days: Government sourcesPetrol, diesel prices may rise by Rs 4–5 per litre and LPG by Rs 40–50 per cylinder, following a sharp jump in global crude oil prices driven by West Asia war.

Will your minimum pension rise from Rs 1,000 to Rs 7,500? Find out who benefitsPensioners across the country may soon see a shift in their monthly income if the proposed changes go through. Here's what we know so far and who could benefit.

8th Pay Commission to visit Hyderabad, Srinagar, Ladakh: What does this mean?The 8th Pay Commission is taking its discussions beyond Delhi, with visits planned to Hyderabad, Srinagar and Ladakh. But what does this outreach mean for government employees and pensioners awaiting revisions?

Paytm sees strong user trust and high merchant stickiness post PPBL actionBrokerage firms tracking the company said the RBI action on PPBL has limited direct impact on Paytm's core business operations, given the structural separation between the listed entity and the payments bank.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

From petrol to food, why could everything get costlier togetherA mix of higher oil prices, a weaker rupee and monsoon risks is putting pressure on India's economy. The overlap could lift household costs, limit policy choices and raise stagflation concerns.

GST at record Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April, but domestic demand growth slowsA major part of April's growth came from import-related revenues. GST collected on imports rose sharply by 25.8% to Rs 57,580 crore.

Workplace injury claims jump 31%: Are businesses doing enough?On Labour Day, as conversations around worker safety and rights take centre stage, a pressing question emerges—are businesses truly doing enough to protect their workforce? A recent report suggests concerns are growing.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

entertainment.headline: Usha Mangeshkar says she feels lonely at home after sisters Lata and Asha's death Usha Mangeshkar spoke about the shock of losing sisters Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle. She said the silence at home has deepened her grief and loneliness.

Patriot box office Day 1: Mohanlal-Mammootty film posts strong global debut Patriot opened strongly in theatres, earning Rs 29.37 crore worldwide on its first day. The Mohanlal and Mammootty reunion, backed by solid Kerala and overseas numbers, drove the opening.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 1: Riteish's film records strong Marathi opening Raja Shivaji opened strongly on Maharashtra Day, collecting around Rs 11.35 crore domestically. Led by its Marathi version, the film set a new opening benchmark and drew solid occupancy across the state.

RGV says Riteish Deshmukh looks like Shivaji reincarnated, praises box office run Ram Gopal Varma has praised Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji after its theatrical release and strong start at the box office. His post highlighted Riteish's work as both director and actor while underscoring the film's early theatrical momentum.

Does Orry shower with Bisleri? His cheeky reply on influencer's reel sparks buzz Orry reacted with a cheeky comment to an influencer's reel claiming that a Bollywood actor bathes only in Bisleri water on set. His response sparked online speculation, amused reactions, and curiosity about the actor.

KJo goes gaga over The Devil Wears Prada 2: This sequel speaks of our time Karan Johar shared an Instagram note after watching The Devil Wears Prada 2 in theatres. He said the sequel revives nostalgia while reflecting anxieties around journalism, print and corporate power.

Lights, camera, vlog! Why Bollywood and TV celebs are turning YouTubers lately YouTube has emerged as a popular platform for many celebrities in the showbiz world to garner fans and big money. What began as a lockdown experiment for many has now evolved into a powerful space to connect and tell stories, through vlogs and podcasts.
Munawar Faruqui, wife Mehzabeen welcome first child Munawar Faruqui and Mehzabeen Faruqui have welcomed their first child together. The couple got married in May 2024.

Producer Firoz Nadiadwala files fraud case over Hera Pheri rights, claims extortion Film producer Firoz Nadiadwala has filed a fraud complaint in Mumbai over the copyright and remake rights of Hera Pheri. He has alleged an attempt to usurp his rights, extort money and disrupt his company's public listing.

Gladiators: Ajith Kumar's racing film first glimpse out on 55th birthday Ajith Kumar's racing film Gladiators – In Pursuit of Challenges unveiled its first glimpse on his 55th birthday. It brings his training and team to theatres while spotlighting his international motorsport campaign.

Raja Shivaji vs Ek Din vs Devil Wears Prada 2: Do holidays still aide box office? The May 1 holiday has split screens between Raja Shivaji, The Devil Wears Prada 2 and Ek Din. Trade experts say the date can lift openings, but audiences now decide a film's fate far faster.

Nandamuri Balakrishna's next announced with Devara director Koratala Siva Director Koratala Siva has announced a new film with Nandamuri Balakrishna, the producers confirmed. The move shifts attention from Siva's upcoming directorial, Devara: Part 2, starring Balakrishna's nephew, Jr NTR.

Diljit Dosanjh brings specially-abled fan on stage, sings Hass Hass for her Diljit Dosanjh brought a specially-abled fan on stage and sang Hass Hass for her during a recent concert in Canada. The video drew warm reactions online.

Hrithik Roshan's birthday poem for son Hridaan is love-wrapped in emotion Hrithik Roshan and Sussanne Khan shared special posts as their son Hridaan turned 18. Hrithik revived a 2021 poem that described his emotions as a proud father.

Swapped review: Netflix's animated film is lost in safe storytellingRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Netflix's animated film Swapped follows Olly and Ivy after a magical plant forces them into each other's bodies. The film pairs handsome visuals and committed voice work with a body-swap story that stays resolutely safe.

Ram Charan's Peddi finally locks a release date in June. Read details here The makers of Peddi have announced June 2026 as the film's release window. Directed by Buchi Babu Sana, the film is headlined by Ram Charan and stars Janhvi Kapoor opposite him.

Glory review: A sports thriller that is ambitious, but not quite a knockoutRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Glory, now streaming on Netflix, is a boxing drama that looks beyond the ring to explore family, ambition and inherited pressure. It blends strong performances with a layered story, but it isn't flawless.

Oscar winner's trophy goes missing after US airport security flags it as 'weapon' A Russian filmmaker's Oscar statuette was confiscated by US airport security, who deemed it a potential weapon, leading to its disappearance after being checked into Lufthansa's hold.

Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal trailer stuck with censor board a week before release The trailer of Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal is awaiting clearance from the censor board despite repeated revisions. With the film due in cinemas on May 8, the delay has left the makers anxious.

general news.headline: Protest erupts in Bengal's Falta; locals allege threat of arson, violence by TMCTension gripped Falta as residents protested alleged threats by TMC cadres, demanded a repoll, and called for CAPF deployment, while heavy security was put in place to maintain order.

EC circular not incorrect: SC setback for Mamata Banerjee before Bengal resultsThe TMC had moved the apex court on Friday seeking the appointment of state government nominees as counting supervisors, alleging this was being denied. The party argued that central government employees could be influenced by the ruling party at the Centre, affecting the level playing field during counting of votes.

War with US will likely resume: Iran claims Washington not committed to agreementsIran's armed forces said a US-Israeli war on the country was likely to resume. The statement underscored rising tensions and Tehran's claim that Washington is not honouring agreements.

Why Mamata is pressing the EVM panic buttonThe Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress, which rarely raised questions on alleged EVM malfunctions, has sounded the alarm over "EVM tampering and swapping". Is it caution, strategy, or a sign of nervousness ahead of counting in Bengal on May 4?

Forget Rs 40 crore retirement fund, Indian students can't even save Rs 3 croreHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's Rs 40 crore retirement estimate by Sandeep Jethwani may be mathematically sound, but salary data tells a different story. With most graduates earning modest incomes and job stability still a concern, reports from PLFS, Unstop, and income surveys reveal a widening gap between what Indians earn and what they need to retire.

4-year-old Pune girl sexually assaulted, murdered by 65-year-old manPolice said a four-year-old girl was allegedly sexually assaulted and murdered in Pune district, and a 65-year-old man with a criminal record has been arrested. Locals staged protests, demanding strict punishment for the accused.

Went to India's dressing room: Pak captain Agha recalls Asia Cup handshake rowSalman Ali Agha revealed that the Pakistan team went to meet Indian players and shake hands with them but found the dressing room door to remain shut. India and Pakistan have maintained the no-handshake policy since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Live: Magrahat Paschim records 56.33%, Diamond Harbour 54.9% turnout till 1 pmWest Bengal Re-Polling Live Updates: The Election Commission ordered re-polling today at 15 booths across Diamond Harbour and Magrahat Paschim on May 2 in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas after allegations of EVM tampering, including claims that buttons were covered with tape. Voting will take place from 7 am to 6 pm, with officials invoking provisions to void the earlier April 29 polling amid complaints of booth jamming and rigging.
At this GK gathering, Delhi's best-dressed women are ditching fast fashionIn GK II, four women are hosting a 'Vintage Xchange' where nothing is bought or sold. Wondering what that is? Read here

Dravidian Defiance, Delhi's Derailed DreamsIt was a period where the high-decibel drama of the titans was replaced by the low-frequency hum of administrative machinery. The state had lived for decades under leaders who were institutions unto themselves. It would now learn to operate under leaders who had to build those institutions again. The era of the solitary oak was over; the season of the collective orchard had begun.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Sandeep Pathak faces likely arrest in Punjab days after quitting AAP for BJPPunjab Police said it filed the cases in two separate districts, with sources indicating that the FIRs include non-bailable charges related to corruption and harassment of women.

India didn't abandon Chabahar dream. It's playing long game with eye on TrumpHandcrafted ByOur Editors For India, the strategic Chabahar port project was a dream, allowing it to bypass Pakistan and reach Afghanistan and Central Asia. Now, those hopes are on the brink after a US sanctions waiver expired. But India seems to have a trick up its sleeve. Read on to find out.

UP woman catches husband with men on hidden cam; alleges threats, dowry demandA woman in Mainpuri has accused her husband of dowry harassment, abuse, and engaging in "unnatural" acts with men, citing hidden camera footage in an FIR filed on April 29. Police have registered a case under relevant sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and Dowry Prohibition Act and launched an investigation

Who is Shivon Zilis, a Punjabi, mother to Elon Musk kids, witness in OpenAI trial?In the ongoing high-stakes battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over OpenAI, the name of Shivon Zilis was mentioned within minutes the trial opened in a US court. But who is Zilis, who also happens to have Punjabi connection.

Sam Altman admits current jobs will go away, says people will have best lifeSam Altman has admitted that many current jobs will disappear as AI grows, but says new roles will emerge in the future. He believes people could still enjoy a more fulfilling and prosperous life in the AI era.

Woman thrown off scooter after hitting raised sewer cover while overtakingA young woman riding a scooter fell on a road in Punjab after losing control while overtaking. The viral dashcam footage has refocused attention on speeding, road hazards and civic maintenance.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

RGV says Riteish Deshmukh looks like Shivaji reincarnated, praises box office runRam Gopal Varma has praised Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji after its theatrical release and strong start at the box office. His post highlighted Riteish's work as both director and actor while underscoring the film's early theatrical momentum.

India tests emergency alert SMS that works without SIM card: ExplainedMillions of users across India received a loud government test alert on phones as part of a new disaster warning system. Here is how it works, why some phones got it without a SIM card, and why others may have missed it.

Lights, camera, vlog! Why Bollywood and TV celebs are turning YouTubers latelyYouTube has emerged as a popular platform for many celebrities in the showbiz world to garner fans and big money. What began as a lockdown experiment for many has now evolved into a powerful space to connect and tell stories, through vlogs and podcasts.

Video shows final moments of mother clutching son before Jabalpur cruise capsizedA viral video shows a mother holding her four-year-old son moments before a cruise capsized in Madhya Pradesh's Jabalpur. Their bodies were later found wrapped in the same life jacket.

Strongroom opened at least 10 times: Now BJP alleges wrongdoing after TMCBJP leaders have complained to the Bidhannagar SDO over the alleged unauthorised opening of a strong room. The complaint has been followed by the suspension of six officials.

Pollsters have a Mamata challenge in Bengal. Will they prove her wrong?At least three exit polls have said that the BJP has an edge over Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress in the Assembly election. Mamata has had a habit of proving pollsters wrong, again and again. Will they prove her wrong this time?

sports.headline: Went to India's dressing room: Pak captain Agha recalls Asia Cup handshake row Salman Ali Agha revealed that the Pakistan team went to meet Indian players and shake hands with them but found the dressing room door to remain shut. India and Pakistan have maintained the no-handshake policy since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Why did Kohli go unpunished? Fans back Jamieson over Sooryavanshi celebration Kyle Jamieson was warned and given a demerit point after his charged send-off to 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the IPL. The episode reopened questions over how the league polices aggression, celebration and consistency in its conduct rules.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi at 15 is the big prize and Jamieson's send-off proves it Kyle Jamieson dismissed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with a yorker and gave him a fiery send-off in Jaipur. Rajasthan Royals batting coach Vikram Rathour said the reaction showed how prized the 15-year-old's wicket has become in cricket.

Can India beat giant-slayers France? All you need to know about Thomas Cup semis Thomas Cup 2026 semi-finals: India will have their work cut out on Saturday, May 2, as they take on giant-killers France in the semi-final of the Thomas Cup 2026. With a strong singles line-up, France will be up for the test but India will once again bank on Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Satwik-Chirag to step up and deliver the win.

PSG-Bayern, What a disaster game: Guardiola attends Stockport game, mocks UCL clash Pep Guardiola joked about skipping PSG vs Bayern's 9-goal thriller, calling it a "disaster game" as he instead watched Stockport County in League One. He also brushed off fixture congestion talk as Manchester City chase Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

Josh Inglis back for bottom-of-the-table LSG, boosts morale ahead of MI clash IPL 2026: Jos Inglis has reportedly rejoined Lucknow Super Giants before their clash with Mumbai Indians. His return comes as LSG battle poor form with Matthew Breetzke heading home for personal reasons.

Train, post and repeat: A survival guide for India's up-and-coming athletes For India's unsung athletes, fame is rare, and sponsorships are rarer. The ones you have never heard of are finding a new way to survive, one post at a time.

CSK vs MI preview: Hardik Pandya under fire as Mumbai Indians seek Chepauk revenge Mumbai Indians travel to Chepauk to face Chennai Super Kings with Hardik Pandya under scrutiny. The match puts two struggling five-time champions under pressure as the season nears an awkward finish.

Sooryavanshi send-off costs Jamieson: DC star penalised for RR game flashpoint Kyle Jamieson was warned and given a demerit point after an aggressive send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Jaipur. The episode triggered criticism online towards Jamieson for the way he celebrated after taking Sooryavanshi's wicket.

KL Rahul 2.0: From strike-rate sceptic to becoming a modern T20 believer KL Rahul said he has worked on his strike-rate after scoring 75 off 40 in Delhi Capitals' win over Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur. His numbers this season, and his comments on modern T20 batting, underline a marked shift in how he approaches the format now after once calling strike-rate overrated.

IPL Play of the Day: Mitchell Starc's big-game aura saw DC cash in on experience Mitchell Starc's well-timed strikes broke RR's momentum at key moments. His experience shaped DC's comeback win in Jaipur.

Mumbai Indians trust Suryakumar Yadav: Coach Jayawardane backs struggling starMahela Jayawardene has backed Suryakumar Yadav despite the batter's lean IPL 2026 run ahead of Mumbai Indians' match against Chennai Super Kings. With Mumbai ninth after two wins in eight matches, the side need a quick turnaround and are relying on senior players to deliver.

Riyan Parag unfazed after vaping row, says he does not have to answer criticsRR vs DC, IPL 2026: Riyan Parag gave a defiant response following his side's defeat to Delhi Capitals in Jaipur. The RR captain believes that he is not answerable to his critics after his recent incident where he was seen vaping in the team dressing room.

Axar Patel dedicates DC win vs RR to fans who died in Delhi road accidentAxar Patel dedicated Delhi Capitals' win over Rajasthan Royals to two fans killed in a road accident after the RCB match. The tribute followed DC's record chase in Jaipur and the captain emphasised on the personal loss felt by the franchise.

Riyan Parag's heroics in vain as Delhi Capitals break the shackles with record chaseIPL 2026, RR vs DC: Delhi Capitals snapped their three-match losing streak on Friday, beating the Rajasthan Royals by seven wickets in Jaipur after recording their highest run-chase in the history of the tournament.

3 T20 World Cup winning captains in 1 frame: Dhoni, Rohit, Suryakumar reuniteIPL 2026: MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav were photographed together during a practice session before the CSK vs MI match at Chepauk. The image drew attention as India's three T20 World Cup-winning captains head into IPL 2026 with injury and form concerns.

Jannik Sinner surpasses Big 3 to create history with Madrid Open final berthJannik Sinner continued his dominant 2026 form by defeating Arthur Fils 6-2, 6-4 to reach the Madrid Open final, becoming the youngest player ever to qualify for the final of all nine ATP Masters 1000 tournaments.

Harshit Rana, Akash Deep expected to miss Afghanistan Test and England tourAkash Deep and Harshit Rana are unlikely to be fit for India's Afghanistan series and UK white-ball tour. Their rehabilitation is progressing, but August's Duleep Trophy looks a more realistic return point.

Riyan Parag puts vaping row behind, steps up with fiery 90 in RR vs DCIPL 2026: Riyan Parag anchored Rajasthan Royals with a composed 90 against Delhi Capitals after an early collapse. The innings shifted attention from his lean run and recent vaping controversy to his control under pressure.

Why MS Dhoni doesn't stay at venues in IPL 2026? Michael Hussey revealsIPL 2026, CSK vs MI: Michael Hussey revealed that MS Dhoni is not attending IPL 2026 matches at venues as he feels his presence could distract the team, with excessive crowd and media attention shifting focus away from CSK's performance.

Confused start, strong finish: How Lakshya Sen rallied back to beat Chou Tien-chenLakshya Sen staged a gritty comeback to defeat Chou Tien-chen 18-21, 22-20, 21-17 in the Thomas Cup quarterfinal, recovering from a game down to give India a 1-0 lead. The win set the tone for India's 3-0 triumph over Chinese Taipei and semifinal qualification.

Sooryavanshi shown no mercy: Kyle Jamieson gives fiery send-off to RR prodigyRR vs DC, IPL 2026: Kyle Jamieson's fiery send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stole the spotlight in Jaipur after the Delhi Capitals quick dismissed the Rajasthan Royals teenager with a sharp yorker.

Starc makes IPL comeback for big RR vs DC clash, but David Miller pays the priceIPL 2026: Mitchell Starc returned for Delhi Capitals against Rajasthan Royals as Axar Patel confirmed three changes at the toss. His comeback and David Miller's tactical omission sharpened focus on Delhi's bid to stop their slide.

Riyan Parag vaping row: Ashwin sympathises, but says action was needed on RR skipperIPL 2026, RR vs DC: R Ashwin expressed empathy for Riyan Parag over the vaping incident during the RR vs PBKS IPL clash, but said the Rajasthan Royals skipper still deserved to be penalised for his actions.

Sunrisers Hyderabad rope in U19 star as injury replacement for Shivam MaviIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad signed India U19 all-rounder RS Ambrish as Shivam Mavi's replacement, with the teenager becoming their latest injury-enforced addition this season.

Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty star as India crush Chinese Taipei to reach semisThomas Cup 2026: India booked their place in the semifinals of the tournament with a dominant 3–0 victory over Chinese Taipei. Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty secured wins in their singles matches, while the doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty triumphed in their match to power India into the top four.

I promise you will be on the floor: SRH's Heinrich Klaasen warns fan over filming familyIPL 2026: A viral video showed Sunrisers Hyderabad batter Heinrich Klaasen warning a fan to stop filming him and his family over privacy concerns. The exchange stirred fresh debate over privacy, cameras in public spaces and the limits of fandom.

Homework hero: Why Ian Bishop goes beyond stats in IPL commentaryIPL 2026: Long before he steps into the IPL commentary box, Ian Bishop is already at work, piecing together stories from research and reportage to bring out the human journeys behind cricket's brightest talents.

F1 returns to Miami with Antonelli on rise and Verstappen's future in doubtMiami GP: Formula 1 returns to Miami with five pressing questions after an unscheduled spring break. Antonelli, Verstappen, Norris and the FIA all arrive under pressure in a season still rapidly taking shape.

India vs Chinese Taipei Highlights: Ayush beats Lin Chun-Yi, India storm to semisThomas Cup 2026 Highlights, India vs Chinese Taipei: Aysuh Shetty beats Lin Chun-Yi by 21-16, 21-17, and that seals India spot in the semi-finals. Previously, Satwik and Chirag gave India a 2-0 lead in the quarterfinals. They beat Chiu Hsiang Chieh and Wang Chi-Lin 23-21, 19-21, 21-12. Earlier, Lakshya Sen completed a comeback to beat Chinese Taipei's Chou Tien Chen 18-21, 22-20, 21-17.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
